members.name: _HandleMenuSelected
members.summary: This virtual method can be implemented to handle context menu items not handled by default. See [method _set_create_options].

members.name: _SetCreateOptions
members.summary: This virtual method is called when updating the context menu of an [member editable] [EditorResourcePicker]. Implement this method to override the "New" items section with your own options. [param menu_node] is a reference to the [PopupMenu] node. [b]Note:[/b] Implement [method _handle_menu_selected] to handle these custom items. [b]Note:[/b] Relevant built-in options ("Load", "Copy", "Paste", etc.) are automatically added to the [param menu_node] afterwards, using their hard-coded IDs starting from [code]0[/code]. Custom options need to use non-colliding IDs to be handled properly. Using [code]id = 100 + custom_option_index[/code] is safe (this is what the default items in the "New" section use).

members.name: GetAllowedTypes
members.summary: Returns a list of all allowed types and subtypes corresponding to the [member base_type]. If the [member base_type] is empty, an empty list is returned.

members.name: SetTogglePressed
members.summary: Sets the toggle mode state for the main button. Works only if [member toggle_mode] is set to [code]true[/code].

members.name: BaseType
members.summary: The base type of allowed resource types. Can be a comma-separated list of several options.

members.name: Editable
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the value can be selected and edited.

members.name: ToggleMode
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the main button with the resource preview works in the toggle mode. Use [method set_toggle_pressed] to manually set the state.

signals.name: ResourceChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the value of the edited resource was changed.

signals.name: ResourceSelected
signals.summary: Emitted when the resource value was set and user clicked to edit it. When [param inspect] is [code]true[/code], the signal was caused by the context menu "Edit" or "Inspect" option.

virtualMethods.name: _HandleMenuSelected
virtualMethods.summary: This virtual method can be implemented to handle context menu items not handled by default. See [method _set_create_options].

Implement this method to override the "New" items section with your own options. [param menu_node] is a reference to the [PopupMenu] node. [b]Note:[/b] Implement [method _handle_menu_selected] to handle these custom items. [b]Note:[/b] Relevant built-in options ("Load", "Copy", "Paste", etc.) are automatically added to the [param menu_node] afterwards, using their hard-coded IDs starting from [code]0[/code].
